artifact.\"\"\"\n    path: Path\n    content: str\n    mtime: float\n    last_check: float\n\n\nclass HotReloadManager:\n    \"\"\"\n    Manages hot-reload for all artifacts.\n\n    Usage:\n        reload_mgr = HotReloadManager()\n\n        # Register artifacts\n        reload_mgr.register_prompt('router', 'prompts/router.md')\n        reload_mgr.register_config('registry', self._cache: Dict[str, Any] = {}  # Parsed content cache\n        self._parsers: Dict[str, Callable[[str], Any]] = {\n            '.md': lambda x: x,\n            '.yaml': self._parse_yaml,\n            '.yml': self._parse_yaml,\n        }\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _parse_yaml(content: str) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Parse YAML content.\"\"\"\n        return yaml.safe_load(content)\n\n    def register_prompt(self, name: str, path: str):\n        \"\"\"\n        Register a prompt artifact for hot-reload.\n\n        Args:\n            name: Artifact name (e.g., 'router', 'synthesize')\n            path: Path to the prompt file\n        \"\"\"\n        full_path = Path(path).expanduser().absolute()\n        if not full_path.exists():\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Prompt file not found: {full_path}\")\n\n        mtime = full_path.stat().st_mtime\n        with open(full_path) as f:\n            content = f.read()\n\n        self._artifacts[name] = Artifact(\n file\n        \"\"\"\n        full_path = Path(path).expanduser().absolute()\n        if not full_path.exists():\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Config file not found: {full_path}\")\n\n _check_and_reload(self, name: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if an artifact needs reloading and reload if so.\n\n        Returns:\n            True if reloaded, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        if name not in self._artifacts:\n            return False\n\n        artifact = self._artifacts[name]\n        now = time.time()\n\n        # Throttle checks\n        if now - artifact.last_check < self.CHECK_INTERVAL:\n            return False\n\n        # Check mtime\n        current_mtime = artifact.path.stat().st_mtime\n        if current_mtime <= artifact.mtime:\n            artifact.last_check = now\n            return False\n\n        # Reload\n        with open(artifact.path) as f:\n            new_content = f.read()\n\n        artifact.content = new_content\n        artifact.mtime = current_mtime\n        artifact.last_check = now\n\n        # Parse and cache\n        suffix = artifact.path.suffix.lower()\n        parser = self._parsers.get(suffix)\n        if parser:\n            self._cache[name] = parser(new_content)\n        else:\n            self._cache[name] = new_content\n\n        return True\n\n    def get_prompt(self, name: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get a prompt's content, reloading if changed.\n\n        Args:\n            name: The prompt artifact name\n\n        Returns:\n            The prompt content as a string\n        \"\"\"\n        self._check_and_reload(name)\n        return self._cache[name]\n\n    def get_config(self, name: str) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get a config's parsed content, reloading if changed.\n\n        Args:\n            name: The config artifact name\n\n        Returns:\n            The parsed config (dict for YAML)\n        \"\"\"\n        self._check_and_reload(name)\n        return self._cache[name]\n\n    def force_reload(self, name: str):\n        \"\"\"\n        Force reload an artifact, bypassing the mtime check.\n\n        Useful after manual edits or in tests.\n        \"\"\"\n        if name not in self._artifacts:\n            raise KeyError(f\"Unknown artifact: {name}\")\n\n        artifact = self._artifacts[name]\n\n        with open(artifact.path) as f:\n            new_content = f.read()\n\n        artifact.content = new_content\n        artifact.mtime = artifact.path.stat().st_mtime\n        artifact.last_check = time.time()\n\n        # Parse and cache\n        suffix = artifact.path.suffix.lower()\n        parser = self._parsers.get(suffix)\n        if parser:\n            self._cache[name] = parser(new_content)\n        else:\n            self._cache[name] = new_content\n\n    def get_mtime(self, name: str) -> Optional[float]:\n        \"\"\"Get the current mtime of an artifact.\"\"\"\n        if name in self._artifacts:\n            return self._artifacts[name].mtime\n        return None\n\n    def list_artifacts(self) -> Dict[str, str]:\n        \"\"\"List all registered artifacts and their paths.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            name: str(artifact.path)\n            for name, artifact in self._artifacts.items()\n        }\n\n\n# Singleton instance for the application\n_reload_manager: Optional[HotReloadManager] = None\n\n\ndef get_reload_manager() -> HotReloadManager:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager singleton.\"\"\"\n    global _reload_manager\n    if _reload_manager is None:\n Surface idle timeout: marks surface as idle after N seconds of inactivity\n45\t    IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 300  # 5 minutes\n46\t\n47\t    def __init__(self, store: SessionStore):\n48\t        self.store = store\n49\t        self._reload_manager = None\n50\t\n51\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n52\t        \"\"\"Get or create hot-reload manager.\"\"\"\n53\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\n54\t            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n55\t        return self._reload_manager\n56\t\n57\t    def _should_route_exception_to_telegram(\n58\t        self,\n59\t        urgency: str,\n60\t        result_type: str = \"result\",\n61\t        session_id: str | None = None,\n62\t    ) -> bool:\n63\t        \"\"\"\n64\t        Check if an exception-class result should route to Telegram.\n65\t\n66\t        Based on exceptions.yaml categories:\n67\t        - Exception-class results (urgency: critical, type: exception)\n68\t        - Push to Telegram if no canvas active within N minutes\n69\t\n70\t        Args:\n71\t            urgency: Result urgency ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')\n72\t            result_type: Result type ('exception', 'result', etc.)\n73\t            session_id: The session ID (for checking canvas activity)\n74\t\n75\t        Returns:\n76\t            True if should route to Telegram, False otherwise\n77\t        \"\"\"\n78\t        # Load exceptions.yaml\n79\t        try:\n80\t            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n81\t            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n82\t        except Exception as e:\n83\t            # If config fails to load, default to False (no exception routing)\n84\t            return False\n85\t\n86\t        # Check categories config for exception routing rules\n87\t        categories = exceptions_config.get(\"categories\", {})\n88\t\n89\t        # Check if this is an exception-class result\n90\t        blocking = categories.get(\"blocking\", {})\n91\t        is_exception_class = (\n92\t            urgency == \"critical\" and\n93\t            result_type == \"exception\"\n94\t        )\n95\t\n96\t        if not is_exception_class:\n97\t            return False\n98\t\n99\t        # Check if auto_push_to_telegram is enabled\n100\t        if not blocking.get(\"auto_push_to_telegram\", False):\n101\t            return False\n102\t\n103\t        # TODO: Check if no canvas has been active within N minutes\n104\t        # This requires tracking canvas activity in the session store\n105\t        # For now, we'll implement a simpler check: no active canvas surfaces\n106\t\n107\t        return True\n108\t\n109\t    async def route_result(\n110\t        self,\n111\t        session_id: str,\n112\t        origin_surface_id: str | None = None,\n113\t        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n114\t        result_type: str = \"result\",\n115\t    ) -> RouteDecision:\n116\t        \"\"\"\n117\t        Determine which surface(s) should receive a result.\n118\t\n119\t        Args:\n120\t            session_id: The session the result belongs to\n121\t            origin_surface_id: The surface where the utterance originated (if known)\n122\t            urgency: Result urgency ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')\n123\t            result_type: Result type ('exception', 'result', etc.)\n124\t\n125\t        Returns:\n126\t            RouteDecision with target surfaces and routing reason\n127\t        \"\"\"\n128\t        # Check for exception-class routing first (critical urgency, type: exception)\n129\t        should_force_telegram = self._should_route_exception_to_telegram(\n130\t            urgency=urgency,\n131\t            result_type=result_type,\n132\t            session_id=session_id,\n133\t        )\n134\t\n135\t        # Get the no-canvas timeout from exceptions.yaml (in seconds)\n136\t        no_canvas_timeout_seconds = self._get_no_canvas_timeout()\n137\t\n138\t        # Check if any canvas has been active within the timeout\n139\t        has_recent_canvas = await self._has_recent_canvas_activity(\n140\t            session_id=session_id,\n141\t            timeout_seconds=no_canvas_timeout_seconds,\n142\t        )\n143\t\n144\t        # Origin surface (if still connected)\n2. Most recently active connected surface\n3. Any connected surface\n4. Always-available fallback (Telegram)\n\nException-class routing (critical urgency, type: exception):\n- Pushes to Telegram if no canvas active within N minutes (from exceptions.yaml)\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nfrom ..components.hot_reload import get_reload_manager\nfrom ..session.store import SessionStore\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass Surface:\n    \"\"\"A surface representation.\"\"\"\n    id: str\n    session_id: str\n    type: str\n    state: str\n    always_available: bool\n    last_seen: int\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RouteDecision:\n    \"\"\"Routing decision for a result.\"\"\"\n    target_surfaces: list[Surface]\n    reason: str\n    fallback_used: bool = False\n\n\nclass SurfaceRouter:\n    \"\"\"Route results to appropriate surfaces based on priority rules.\"\"\"\n\n    # Surface idle timeout: marks surface as idle after N seconds of inactivity\n    IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 300  # 5 minutes\n\n    def __init__(self, store: SessionStore):\n        self.store = store\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create hot-reload manager.\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _should_route_exception_to_telegram(\n        self,\n        urgency: str,\n        result_type: str = \"result\",\n        session_id: str | None = None,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if an exception-class result should route to Telegram.\n\n        Based on exceptions.yaml categories:\n        - Exception-class results (urgency: critical, type: exception)\n        - Push to Telegram if no canvas active within N minutes\n\n        Args:\n            urgency: Result urgency ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')\n            result_type: Result type ('exception', 'result', etc.)\n            session_id: The session ID (for checking canvas activity)\n\n        Returns:\n            True if should route to Telegram, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        # Load exceptions.yaml\n        try:\n            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            # If config fails to load, default to False (no exception routing)\n            return False\n\n        # Check categories config for exception routing rules\n        categories = exceptions_config.get(\"categories\", {})\n\n        # Check if this is an exception-class result\n        blocking = categories.get(\"blocking\", {})\n        is_exception_class = (\n            urgency == \"critical\" and\n            result_type == \"exception\"\n        )\n\n        if not is_exception_class:\n            return False\n\n        # Check if auto_push_to_telegram is enabled\n        if not blocking.get(\"auto_push_to_telegram\", False):\n            return False\n\n        # TODO: Check if no canvas has been active within N minutes\n        # This requires tracking canvas activity in the session store\n        # For now, we'll implement a simpler check: no active canvas surfaces\n\n        return True\n\n    async def route_result(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        origin_surface_id: str | None = None,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n        result_type: str = \"result\",\n    ) -> RouteDecision:\n        \"\"\"\n        Determine which surface(s) should receive a result.\n\n        Args:\n            session_id: The session the result belongs to\n            origin_surface_id: The surface where the utterance originated (if known)\n            urgency: Result urgency ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')\n            result_type: Result type ('exception', 'result', etc.)\n\n        Returns:\n            RouteDecision with target surfaces and routing reason\n        \"\"\"\n        # Check for exception-class routing first (critical urgency, type: exception)\n        should_force_telegram = self._should_route_exception_to_telegram(\n            urgency=urgency,\n            result_type=result_type,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Get the no-canvas timeout from exceptions.yaml (in seconds)\n        no_canvas_timeout_seconds = self._get_no_canvas_timeout()\n\n        # Check if any canvas has been active within the timeout\n        has_recent_canvas = await self._has_recent_canvas_activity(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            timeout_seconds=no_canvas_timeout_seconds,\n        )\n\n        # Force Telegram routing for exceptions with no recent canvas\n        if should_force_telegram and not has_recent_canvas:\n            fallback = await self.store.get_fallback_surface()\n            if fallback:\n                return RouteDecision(\n                    target_surfaces=[Surface(**fallback)],\n                    reason=\"exception-class-no-canvas-telegram-fallback\",\n                    fallback_used=True,\n                )\n\n        active_surfaces = await self.store.get_active_surfaces(session_id)\n\n        # Filter out idle surfaces (but keep always-available ones)\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        recently_active = [\n            s for s in active_surfaces\n            if s[\"always_available\"] or (now - s[\"last_seen\"]) < self.IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS\n        ]\n\n        # Priority 1: Origin surface (if still connected)\n        if origin_surface_id:\n            for surface in recently_active:\n                if surface[\"id\"] == origin_surface_id and surface[\"state\"] != \"disconnected\":\n                    return RouteDecision(\n                        target_surfaces=[Surface(**surface)],\n                        reason=\"origin-surface-active\",\n                    )\n\n        # Priority 2: Most recently active connected surface\n        if recently_active:\n            # Sort by last_seen descending, pick the first non-telegram unless all are telegram\n            recently_active.sort(key=lambda s: s[\"last_seen\"], reverse=True)\n\n            # Prefer non-telegram surfaces for non-critical results\n            if urgency not in (\"critical\", \"high\"):\n                for surface in recently_active:\n                    if surface[\"type\"] != \"telegram\":\n                        return RouteDecision(\n                            target_surfaces=[Surface(**surface)],\n                            reason=\"most-recent-active\",\n                        )\n\n            # For high urgency or if only telegram available\n            return RouteDecision(\n                target_surfaces=[Surface(**recently_active[0])],\n                reason=\"most-recent-active-or-fallback\",\n            )\n\n        # Priority 3: Any connected surface (including idle)\n        if active_surfaces:\n            active_surfaces.sort(key=lambda s: s[\"last_seen\"], reverse=True)\n            return RouteDecision(\n                target_surfaces=[Surface(**active_surfaces[0])],\n                reason=\"any-connected\",\n            )\n\n        # Priority 4: Always-available fallback (Telegram)\n        fallback = await self.store.get_fallback_surface()\n        if fallback:\n            return RouteDecision(\n                target_surfaces=[Surface(**fallback)],\n                reason=\"always-available-fallback\",\n                fallback_used=True,\n            )\n\n        # No surface available - result will wait in queue\n        return RouteDecision(\n            target_surfaces=[],\n            reason=\"no-surface-available\",\n        )\n\n    def _get_no_canvas_timeout(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the no-canvas timeout from exceptions.yaml.\n\n        Returns the timeout in seconds. Defaults to 10 minutes if not configured.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n\n            # Get categories config\n            categories = exceptions_config.get(\"categories\", {})\n            blocking = categories.get(\"blocking\", {})\n\n            # Get no_canvas_timeout_minutes (default to 10 minutes)\n            timeout_minutes = blocking.get(\"no_canvas_timeout_minutes\", 10)\n\n            return timeout_minutes * 60  # Convert to seconds\n        except Exception:\n            # Default to 10 minutes (600 seconds)\n            return 600\n\n    async def _has_recent_canvas_activity(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        timeout_seconds: int,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if any canvas surface has been active within the timeout.\n\n        Args:\n            session_id: The session to check\n            timeout_seconds: The timeout in seconds\n\n        Returns:\n            True if canvas activity within timeout, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            active_surfaces = await self.store.get_active_surfaces(session_id)\n            now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n            # Check for any canvas (non-telegram) surface active within timeout\n            for surface in active_surfaces:\n                # Skip always-available surfaces (like Telegram)\n                if surface.get(\"always_available\"):\n                    continue\n\n                # Check if surface is a canvas (not telegram)\n                if surface.get(\"type\") == \"telegram\":\n                    continue\n\n                # Check if surface was active within timeout\n                last_seen = surface.get(\"last_seen\", 0)\n                if now - last_seen < timeout_seconds:\n                    return True\n\n            return False\n        except Exception as e:\n            # On error, assume no recent canvas (safer to route to Telegram)\n            return False\n\n    async def route_to_all_active(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        exclude_surface_id: str | None = None,\n    ) -> list[Surface]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route to all active surfaces (for multi-surface scenarios).\n\n        Used when a user has both canvas and audio surfaces active.\n        \"\"\"\n        active_surfaces = await self.store.get_active_surfaces(session_id)\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n        result = []\n        for s in active_surfaces:\n            if exclude_surface_id and s[\"id\"] == exclude_surface_id:\n                continue\n            if s[\"always_available\"] or (now - s[\"last_seen\"]) < self.IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS:\n                result.append(Surface(**s))\n\n        return result\n\n    async def handle_surface_disconnect(self, surface_id: str, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle a surface disconnecting.\n        Marks surface as disconnected and cleans up if needed.\n        \"\"\"\n        await self.store.mark_surface_disconnected(surface_id)\n\n    async def handle_surface_reconnect(\n        self,\n        surface_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle a surface reconnecting after disconnection.\n\n        Returns True if this is a reconnection of an existing surface,\n        False if it's a new surface.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Check if surface exists\n        async with self.store as store:\n            surfaces = await store.get_active_surfaces(session_id)\n            for existing in surfaces:\n                if existing[\"id\"] == surface_id:\n                    # Reconnection - update heartbeat\n                    await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n                    return True\n\n        # New surface - register it\n        await store.register_surface(session_id, surface_type)\n        return False\n\n    async def get_workload_summary(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get workload summary for a session (for surface reconnection).\n        \"\"\"\n        return await self.store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n","numLines":335,"startLine":1,"totalLines":335}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_3072437b8988430d85869e32","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# aide-de-camp: Application Plan\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\taide-de-camp is a universal personal interface that accepts stream-of-consciousness voice and text input, routes it to parallel agents across any domain, and renders results as a live canvas of HTML cards or as synthesized audio narration. It is built on top of existing personal infrastructure and improves itself through conversational feedback after initial bootstrap.\n6\t\n7\tCLI invocation: `adc`\n8\t\n9\t---\n10\t\n11\t## Problem Statement\n12\t\n13\tManaging multiple active projects requires constant context-switching between dedicated tabs, terminals, and chat sessions. The input channel (typing into a specific Claude Code tab) is also the routing layer, and that conflation adds cognitive overhead. The same problem extends beyond coding: research, lookups, personal context, reminders — anything that requires knowing which system to ask before you can ask it.\n14\t\n15\taide-de-camp eliminates that overhead by providing a single input surface that routes automatically, dispatches in parallel, and returns organized results without requiring the user to know where to ask.\n16\t\n17\t---\n18\t\n19\t## Core Architecture\n20\t\n21\t### The Hot Path\n22\t\n23\t```\n24\tUser utterance (voice or text)\n25\t  → STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n26\t  → Intent Router (one LLM call, haiku-class, ~500ms)\n27\t      output: [{project_slug, intent_type, urgency, utterance_fragment}, ...]\n28\t  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize workers (one per intent thread)\n29\t      Fetch: deterministic code, executes command matrix based on intent_type\n30\t      Synthesize: one LLM call per thread (sonnet-class, ~1-2s)\n31\t      output: {data, summary, urgency}\n32\t  → Results streamed via SSE to active canvas\n33\t  → Cards rendered client-side using component templates\n34\t```\n35\t\n36\tTarget: < 3 seconds from utterance to first partial result on canvas. No Claude Code session startup on this path.\n37\t\n38\t### The Async Path\n39\t\n40\t```\n41\tTask-profile intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n42\t  → NEEDLE task bead created (br create)\n43\t  → Existing Claude Code workers pick up bead normally\n44\t  → Bead watcher detects closure event\n45\t  → Result written to session store results table\n46\t  → SSE push to active canvas (or Telegram if no canvas)\n47\t```\n48\t\n49\t### The Self-Improvement Path\n50\t\n51\t```\n52\tFeedback signal (explicit instruction or implicit engagement pattern)\n53\t  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE task bead)\n54\t      reads target artifact (prompt file, registry YAML, component template)\n55\t      generates update\n56\t      surfaces diff to user\n57\t  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded on next invocation\n58\t```\n59\t\n60\t---\n61\t\n62\t## Components\n63\t\n64\t### 1. Intent Router\n65\t\n66\t**Runtime:** Direct API call (haiku-class via ZAI proxy)\n67\t\n68\tOne LLM call per utterance. Receives the full utterance text, the project registry, and a segmentation prompt. Returns a JSON array of intent threads, each tagged with:\n69\t\n70\t```json\n71\t[\n72\t  {\n73\t    \"project_slug\": \"options-pipeline\",\n74\t    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n75\t    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n76\t    \"utterance_fragment\": \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n77\t  },\n78\t  {\n79\t    \"project_slug\": \"ibkr-mcp\",\n80\t    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n81\t    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n82\t    \"utterance_fragment\": \"what's the state of the ibkr mcp\"\n83\t  }\n84\t]\n85\t```\n86\t\n87\tIntent types: `status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`\n88\t\n89\tThe router reads its segmentation prompt and the project registry from disk on each call — no caching. Hot-reload is automatic.\n90\t\n91\tAmbiguous intents: if confidence is below threshold, router returns an `intent_type: \"clarification\"` thread. The voice model or canvas handles the clarification round-trip before dispatching.\n92\t\n93\t### 2. Project Registry\n94\t\n95\t**Format:** YAML file, read per-invocation by the router\n96\t\n97\tDefines projects the router knows about:\n98\t\n99\t```yaml\n100\tprojects:\n101\t  options-pipeline:\n102\t    aliases: [\"the pipeline\", \"options\"]\n103\t    description: \"Options data pipeline on apexalgo-iad\"\n104\t    cluster: apexalgo-iad\n105\t    namespace: options\n106\t    intent_support: [status, action, brainstorm]\n107\t    workflows:\n108\t      deploy:\n109\t        steps: [ci-status, image-tag, argocd-sync, pod-status]\n110\t\n111\t  ibkr-mcp:\n112\t    aliases: [\"ibkr\", \"the mcp\"]\n113\t    description: \"IBKR MCP server\"\n114\t    cluster: apexalgo-iad\n115\t    namespace: ibkr-mcp\n116\t    intent_support: [status, brainstorm, lookup]\n117\t```\n118\t\n119\tUpdated by the self-modification agent. Hot-reloaded on every router call.\n120\t\n121\t### 3. Fetch Strand\n122\t\n123\t**Runtime:** Deterministic code, no LLM\n124\t\n125\tExecutes a command matrix based on `intent_type` and the project's registry entry. No LLM decisions — the commands are determined by the intent type and project config.\n126\t\n127\tExample command matrix for `intent_type: status`:\n128\t\n129\t| Source | Command |\n130\t|--------|---------|\n131\t| Pod status | `kubectl get pods -n {namespace}` |\n132\t| ArgoCD sync | `curl argocd-ro-api/applications/{app}` |\n133\t| Git log | `git -C {repo_path} log -10 --oneline` |\n134\t| Bead list | `br list --project {slug} --status open` |\n135\t| CI status | `kubectl get workflows -n argo-workflows -l project={slug}` |\n136\t\n137\tResults are structured data passed to the Synthesize strand. Fetch runs each source concurrently; partial results (from sources that respond first) are passed to Synthesize incrementally to support streaming output.\n138\t\n139\tA `fetch_coverage` field tracks which sources succeeded and which failed. Failed sources are surfaced as caveats in the result.\n140\t\n141\t### 4. Synthesize Strand\n142\t\n143\t**Runtime:** Direct API call (sonnet-class via ZAI proxy)\n144\t\n145\tTakes `{intent_spec, fetched_context}` → produces `{data, summary}`.\n146\t\n147\t- `data`: structured JSON matching the component library's expected schema for this intent type\n148\t- `summary`: 1-3 sentence narration suitable for audio mode\n149\t\n150\tSingle-turn inference. Reads its system prompt from a file per invocation (hot-reload). The synthesize prompt is the highest-leverage artifact in the system — it defines result format, detail level, and what fields to include.\n151\t\n152\tRuns once per intent thread in parallel across all active threads.\n153\t\n154\t### 5. Escalate Strand\n155\t\n156\t**Runtime:** Direct API call + `br create`\n157\t\n158\tFor task-profile intents that need durable async handling:\n159\t1. One LLM call to formulate a bead body from the intent + context\n160\t2. `br create` to create the bead in the appropriate project\n161\t3. Returns a pending-card spec with the bead reference\n162\t\n163\tThe bead watcher subsequently bridges bead closure to result delivery.\n164\t\n165\t### 6. Voice Model\n166\t\n167\t**Runtime:** Realtime API (OpenAI or Claude Realtime, persistent session)\n168\t\n169\tThe conversational layer. Receives transcribed utterances (or audio directly), calls `dispatch_intent()` tool to trigger routing, and narrates results at appropriate moments.\n170\t\n171\tTool calls are triggers, not queries. `dispatch_intent()` returns an acknowledgment immediately; results arrive out-of-band and are surfaced by the voice model at natural conversational pauses.\n172\t\n173\tThe voice model's system prompt controls narration style, batching behavior, urgency handling, and multi-turn topic tracking. Read per session-turn from a file (hot-reload).\n174\t\n175\tIn audio mode there is no canvas. The voice model reads `result.summary` directly. When a user transitions from audio to canvas, pending results are rendered using the component library and appear on canvas — session continuity means the canvas catches up.\n176\t\n177\t### 7. UI-Regen Agent\n178\t\n179\t**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n180\t\n181\tSteward of the component library. Given a result's data shape:\n182\t1. Finds the best-fit existing component (semantic match against component descriptions)\n183\t2. If no good fit, generates a new component from scratch and stores it\n184\t3. Applies the component template to result data accounting for layout bucket\n185\t4. On feedback, iterates the component (updates library, triggers canvas re-render)\n186\t\n187\tMulti-step: reads library, generates or selects, writes back. Claude Code's file access and tool use make this the right runtime.\n188\t\n189\tCreated as a task bead; not on the hot path. Canvas updates in place when a component is versioned (SSE push: `component_updated: {component_id, version}`).\n190\t\n191\t### 8. Self-Modification Agent\n192\t\n193\t**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n194\t\n195\tReads and writes the artifacts that encode system behavior:\n196\t- Router prompt (`prompts/router.md`)\n197\t- Project registry (`config/registry.yaml`)\n198\t- Strand prompts (`prompts/fetch.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, etc.)\n199\t- Urgency classifier prompt (`prompts/urgency.md`)\n200\t- Voice model prompt (`prompts/voice.md`)\n201\t- Monitoring config (`config/monitoring.yaml`)\n202\t- Exception routing rules (`config/exceptions.yaml`)\n203\t\n204\tWorkflow for every modification:\n205\t1. Read current artifact\n206\t2. Understand intent of change\n207\t3. Generate updated artifact\n208\t4. Surface diff to user (in canvas or narrated in audio mode)\n209\t5. On approval: write artifact, hot-reload takes effect on next invocation\n210\t6. On rejection: discard\n211\t\n212\tSafety model:\n213\t- Diffs before every application — no silent changes\n214\t- All artifacts versioned; any update can be rolled back with one instruction\n215\t- Confidence threshold: unambiguous changes (adding an alias) can auto-apply with notification; structural changes always require explicit approval\n216\t\n217\t### 9. Background Analysis Bead\n218\t\n219\t**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead, low priority)\n220\t\n221\tRuns on a schedule. Reads intent history and engagement signals from the session store, identifies patterns, proposes artifact updates.\n222\t\n223\tExamples:\n224\t- \"User consistently drills into status results for more detail → propose adding causality fields to synthesize prompt\"\n225\t- \"User ignores pipeline monitoring pushes for 5 days → propose lowering urgency tier\"\n226\t- \"After deploy status, user always asks about pod logs within 2 minutes → propose speculative pre-fetch\"\n227\t\n228\tProposals are surfaced as cards on canvas for user review. Auto-apply only above a high confidence threshold.\n229\t\n230\t### 10. Bead Watcher\n231\t\n232\t**Runtime:** Daemon process, no LLM\n233\t\n234\tWatches for bead close events from NEEDLE workers. On close:\n235\t1. Reads `session_id` from closed bead metadata\n236\t2. Looks up active surface in session store\n237\t3. Writes result to `results` table\n238\t4. Fires SSE push to canvas or Telegram push if no canvas active\n239\t\n240\tPure I/O. Runs as a long-running process (Deployment, not a K8s Job per infrastructure conventions).\n241\t\n242\t---\n243\t\n244\t## Data Model\n245\t\n246\t### Session Store (SQLite)\n247\t\n248\t```sql\n249\tsessions (\n250\t  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n251\t  created_at  INTEGER,\n252\t  last_active INTEGER,\n253\t  primary_surface_id TEXT\n254\t)\n255\t\n256\tsurfaces (\n257\t  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n258\t  session_id      TEXT,\n259\t  type            TEXT,  -- 'canvas' | 'telegram' | 'audio'\n260\t  state           TEXT,  -- 'active' | 'idle' | 'disconnected'\n261\t  always_available INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- 1 for Telegram\n262\t  last_seen       INTEGER\n263\t)\n264\t\n265\tutterances (\n266\t  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n267\t  session_id  TEXT,\n268\t  raw_text    TEXT,\n269\t  created_at  INTEGER\n270\t)\n271\t\n272\tintents (\n273\t  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n274\t  utterance_id TEXT,\n275\t  session_id   TEXT,\n276\t  topic_id     TEXT,\n277\t  project_slug TEXT,\n278\t  intent_type  TEXT,\n279\t  status       TEXT,  -- 'pending' | 'dispatched' | 'resolved' | 'cancelled'\n280\t  bead_ref     TEXT,  -- set for task-profile intents\n281\t  created_at   INTEGER,\n282\t  resolved_at  INTEGER\n283\t)\n284\t\n285\tresults (\n286\t  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n287\t  intent_id   TEXT,\n288\t  topic_id    TEXT,\n289\t  session_id  TEXT,\n290\t  summary     TEXT,\n291\t  data        TEXT,  -- JSON\n292\t  urgency     TEXT,  -- 'critical' | 'high' | 'normal' | 'low'\n293\t  created_at  INTEGER,\n294\t  surfaced_at INTEGER,\n295\t  acked_at    INTEGER\n296\t)\n297\t\n298\ttopics (\n299\t  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n300\t  label        TEXT,\n301\t  type         TEXT,  -- 'project' | 'research' | 'personal' | 'exception'\n302\t  project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n303\t  scope        TEXT,  -- 'session' | 'cross-session' | 'global'\n304\t  session_id   TEXT,  -- null for cross-session/global\n305\t  created_at   INTEGER,\n306\t  last_active  INTEGER,\n307\t  archived_at  INTEGER\n308\t)\n309\t\n310\tintent_topics (\n311\t  intent_id TEXT,\n312\t  topic_id  TEXT,\n313\t  PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id)\n314\t)\n315\t```\n316\t\n317\t### Component Library (SQLite, separate DB)\n318\t\n319\t```sql\n320\tcomponents (\n321\t  id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n322\t  name          TEXT,\n323\t  description   TEXT,\n324\t  html_template TEXT,\n325\t  version       INTEGER,\n326\t  created_at    INTEGER,\n327\t  last_used     INTEGER,\n328\t  usage_count   INTEGER\n329\t)\n330\t\n331\tcomponent_versions (\n332\t  component_id  TEXT,\n333\t  version       INTEGER,\n334\t  html_template TEXT,\n335\t  created_at    INTEGER,\n336\t  change_note   TEXT,\n337\t  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, version)\n338\t)\n339\t\n340\tcard_cache (\n341\t  result_id         TEXT,\n342\t  component_id      TEXT,\n343\t  component_version INTEGER,\n344\t  layout_bucket     TEXT,  -- 'compact' | 'normal' | 'expanded'\n345\t  rendered_html     TEXT,\n346\t  created_at        INTEGER,\n347\t  PRIMARY KEY (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n348\t)\n349\t```\n350\t\n351\t---\n352\t\n353\t## File System Layout\n354\t\n355\t```\n356\taide-de-camp/\n357\t├── adc                      ← CLI entry point (shell script or compiled binary)\n358\t├── config/\n359\t│   ├── registry.yaml        ← project registry (hot-reloaded by router)\n360\t│   ├── monitoring.yaml      ← ambient monitoring rules\n361\t│   └── exceptions.yaml      ← exception routing rules\n362\t├── prompts/\n363\t│   ├── router.md            ← intent segmentation prompt\n364\t│   ├── synthesize.md        ← result generation prompt\n365\t│   ├── voice.md             ← voice model system prompt\n366\t│   ├── urgency.md           ← urgency classification prompt\n367\t│   └── fetch/               ← per-intent-type fetch instructions\n368\t│       ├── status.md\n369\t│       ├── action.md\n370\t│       └── ...\n371\t├── src/\n372\t│   ├── router/              ← intent segmentation (direct API call)\n373\t│   ├── fetch/               ← fetch strand (deterministic, per intent type)\n374\t│   ├── synthesize/          ← synthesize strand (direct API call)\n375\t│   ├── escalate/            ← escalate strand (bead creation)\n376\t│   ├── watcher/             ← bead watcher daemon\n377\t│   ├── session/             ← session store (SQLite)\n378\t│   ├── canvas/              ← web frontend (SSE consumer, card renderer)\n379\t│   └── cli/                 ← adc CLI\n380\t├── data/\n381\t│   ├── session.db           ← session store\n382\t│   └── components.db        ← component library\n383\t└── docs/\n384\t    ├── research/            ← architecture and design research\n385\t    ├── plan/\n386\t    │   └── plan.md          ← this file\n387\t    └── notes/\n388\t        └── naming.md\n389\t```\n390\t\n391\t---\n392\t\n393\t## Deployment Model\n394\t\n395\taide-de-camp is a **live web application**, not a static site. The FastAPI server is the intelligence layer — it handles SSE connections, maintains session state, runs the router and synthesize strands, and serves the frontend HTML. There is no CDN path (no CF Pages).\n396\t\n397\t### Why not static\n398\t\n399\t- SSE connections are long-lived; CDNs cannot proxy them\n400\t- WebSocket for the Realtime API voice session requires a persistent backend\n401\t- The session store (SQLite) and artifact store (prompts, registry) require a writable filesystem\n402\t- Hot-reload depends on the running server reading updated files from disk on each invocation\n403\t\n404\t### Phased hosting\n405\t\n406\t**Phase 0 (Hetzner server directly)**\n407\t\n408\tRun FastAPI as a process on the Hetzner server itself, not in k8s. Simplest possible path:\n409\t- NEEDLE workers and the aide-de-camp server share the same filesystem\n410\t- Self-modification agent writes directly to `prompts/` and `config/` — hot-reload works without any coordination\n411\t- Expose via Tailscale (the server is already on the mesh); no ingress config needed\n412\t- SQLite DBs are local files; no PVC required\n413\t\n414\tNo container, no CI, no ArgoCD. Just `uvicorn src.main:app` managed by a long-running tmux session or systemd unit.\n415\t\n416\t**Phase 1+ (containerized, ardenone-cluster)**\n417\t\n418\tOnce session persistence and multi-surface routing are needed, containerize and move to k8s:\n419\t\n420\t```\n421\tDocker image: ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp\n422\tDeployment: ardenone-cluster, namespace: aide-de-camp\n423\tPVC: /data/ (SATA, ReadWriteOnce)\n424\t  /data/session.db        ← session store\n425\t  /data/components.db     ← component library\n426\t  /data/prompts/          ← hot-reloadable prompt files\n427\t  /data/config/           ← registry.yaml, monitoring.yaml, exceptions.yaml\n428\t```\n429\t\n430\tThe artifact store (prompts, registry) moves from the repo's working directory to the PVC. The self-modification agent updates artifacts via the aide-de-camp API (`PATCH /artifacts/{name}`), which writes to the PVC path the server reads from.\n431\t\n432\t### Traefik configuration for SSE and WebSocket\n433\t\n434\tTraefik's default response buffering breaks SSE. The IngressRoute needs:\n435\t\n436\t```yaml\n437\t# IngressRoute annotations\n438\ttraefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: aide-de-camp-strip-prefix@kubernetescrd\n439\t\n440\t# Middleware: disable buffering, extend timeouts\n441\tapiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\n442\tkind: Middleware\n443\tmetadata:\n444\t  name: aide-de-camp-streaming\n445\tspec:\n446\t  buffering:\n447\t    maxResponseBodyBytes: 0   # disable buffering\n448\t  headers:\n449\t    customResponseHeaders:\n450\t      X-Accel-Buffering: \"no\"\n451\t```\n452\t\n453\tServersTransport timeout:\n454\t\n455\t```yaml\n456\tapiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\n457\tkind: ServersTransport\n458\tmetadata:\n459\t  name: aide-de-camp-longlived\n460\tspec:\n461\t  forwardingTimeouts:\n462\t    responseHeaderTimeout: 0    # no timeout on SSE streams\n463\t    dialTimeout: 5s\n464\t```\n465\t\n466\tDUCK-E's existing WebSocket IngressRoute config is the reference implementation for this cluster.\n467\t\n468\t### SQLite concurrency\n469\t\n470\tMultiple writers (bead watcher, background analysis bead, session handler) require WAL mode to avoid lock contention:\n471\t\n472\t```python\n473\t# On DB open\n474\tconn.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n475\tconn.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n476\t```\n477\t\n478\tWAL mode allows concurrent reads during writes and serializes multiple writers without blocking. For a personal single-user app, this is sufficient through Phase 3. A server database (Postgres) is not needed.\n479\t\n480\t### adc CLI connectivity\n481\t\n482\tThe `adc` CLI is a thin HTTP client. It talks to the running server over Tailscale:\n483\t\n484\t```bash\n485\t# ~/.config/adc/config\n486\tserver_url = \"http://aide-de-camp.ardenone.com\"   # or localhost in Phase 0\n487\t```\n488\t\n489\tNo local inference. The CLI sends requests to the FastAPI backend and streams the SSE response to the terminal. On the Hetzner server in Phase 0, `server_url = \"http://localhost:8000\"`.\n490\t\n491\t### CI/CD (Phase 1+)\n492\t\n493\tNew Argo WorkflowTemplate `aide-de-camp-build` on iad-ci:\n494\t- Docker build → `ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp`\n495\t- Update image tag in `jedarden/declarative-config` (k8s/ardenone-cluster/aide-de-camp/)\n496\t- ArgoCD syncs automatically\n497\t\n498\tSame pattern as every other containerized service in the stack.\n499\t\n500\t---\n501\t\n502\t## Technology Stack\n503\t\n504\t| Layer | Choice | Reason |\n505\t|-------|--------|--------|\n506\t| Backend | FastAPI (Python) | Reuse DUCK-E scaffolding; SSE + WebSocket support; async-native |\n507\t| STT | Web Speech API → whisper-stt fallback | Zero-latency browser-native; whisper-stt already deployed |\n508\t| Voice session | OpenAI Realtime API (via DUCK-E session handler) | DUCK-E scaffolding already built on this |\n509\t| LLM calls | ZAI proxy (`llm-proxy.ardenone.com`) | Pay-per-token, no subscription pressure on hot path |\n510\t| Task workers | NEEDLE + Claude Code | Existing infrastructure, unchanged |\n511\t| Session store | SQLite (WAL mode) | Lightweight, local, additive-only schema; single-user app |\n512\t| Artifact store | PVC-mounted filesystem (Phase 1+) / local filesystem (Phase 0) | Writable at runtime; hot-reload reads per-invocation |\n513\t| Frontend | Vanilla JS + Web Components | No build pipeline; SSE consumer + client-side card renderer |\n514\t| Phase 0 hosting | Hetzner server, local process | Simplest path; shared filesystem with NEEDLE workers |\n515\t| Phase 1+ hosting | ardenone-cluster, k8s Deployment | Behind existing Traefik + Tailscale ingress |\n516\t\n517\t---\n518\t\n519\t## Implementation Phases\n520\t\n521\t### Phase 0 — Minimal Viable Surface (~2 days)\n522\t\n523\tValidates the core question: does routing + parallel dispatch reduce friction?\n524\t\n525\t- Single HTML page: textarea + mic button (Web Speech API)\n526\t- `POST /dispatch` endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream\n527\t- Results appear as basic cards as agents resolve\n528\t- No persistence, no auth, no component library, no ambient push\n529\t\n530\tDeliverable: the core query loop working end-to-end.\n531\t\n532\t### Phase 1 — Session and Topics (~1 week)\n533\t\n534\tResults persist; the canvas has memory.\n535\t\n536\t- Session store (SQLite, 7 tables)\n537\t- Topic model: canvas shows one card per active topic, updated in place\n538\t- Telegram surface fallback (reuse telegram-claude-bridge)\n539\t- Bead watcher: closed NEEDLE beads push results to active surface\n540\t- Workload summary on reconnect\n541\t- Staleness indicators on cards\n542\t\n543\tDeliverable: sessions that survive browser refresh; Telegram fallback working.\n544\t\n545\t### Phase 2 — Self-Improvement Loop (~2 weeks)\n546\t\n547\tThe interface can be improved by talking into it.\n548\t\n549\t- Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE)\n550\t- Hot-reload confirmed at every artifact layer\n551\t- Component library: UI-regen agent generates first components from actual result shapes\n552\t- Canvas live-updates when a component is versioned\n553\t- Explicit feedback processed: \"always include X\" → prompt updated → hot-reloaded\n554\t\n555\tDeliverable: at least one end-to-end self-modification cycle working (user instructs change, diff surfaced, approved, takes effect without redeploy).\n556\t\n557\t### Phase 3 — Responsiveness (~2-3 weeks)\n558\t\n559\tThe interface feels alive, not just reactive.\n560\t\n561\t- Ambient monitoring: active topics watched for state changes\n562\t- Diff-aware results: show what changed since last result\n563\t- Pre-warmed context: active topics refreshed in background every N minutes\n564\t- Multi-turn within topic: follow-up questions deepen current topic context\n565\t- Speculative pre-fetch: common follow-up patterns pre-fetched\n566\t- Notification batching in audio mode\n567\t- Implicit feedback signals fed to background analysis bead\n568\t\n569\tDeliverable: monitoring fires unprompted for a watched topic; follow-up questions are visibly faster.\n570\t\n571\t### Phase 4 — Audio Surface (~1-2 weeks)\n572\t\n573\tFull audio mode via Realtime API.\n574\t\n575\t- Realtime API voice session replaces text input path\n576\t- Tool-as-trigger model: `dispatch_intent()` returns ack, result arrives async\n577\t- Urgency-tiered voice narration\n578\t- Audio-to-canvas session continuity\n579\t\n580\tDeliverable: full voice session with canvas catch-up on surface switch.\n581\t\n582\t---\n583\t\n584\t## Self-Improvement: Hot-Reload Architecture\n585\t\n586\tEvery artifact that encodes behavior is readable, writable, and reloaded per-invocation:\n587\t\n588\t| Behavior | Artifact | Reload point |\n589\t|----------|----------|-------------|\n590\t| Utterance segmentation | `prompts/router.md` | Each router call |\n591\t| Intent→project routing | `config/registry.yaml` | Each router call |\n592\t| Context fetch strategy | `prompts/fetch/{type}.md` | Each fetch invocation |\n593\t| Result format and detail | `prompts/synthesize.md` | Each synthesize call |\n594\t| Voice narration style | `prompts/voice.md` | Each session turn |\n595\t| Urgency classification | `prompts/urgency.md` | Each escalate call |\n596\t| Visual rendering | Component library (DB) | Each card render |\n597\t| Monitoring rules | `config/monitoring.yaml` | Each monitoring tick |\n598\t\n599\tPer-invocation reload is the implementation strategy (read file, check mtime). File watching adds complexity for no benefit — prompts change rarely and file reads are cheap.\n600\t\n601\t---\n602\t\n603\t## Surface Routing Rules\n604\t\n605\tWhen a result is ready to surface, the bead watcher or synthesize strand uses this priority:\n606\t\n607\t1. The surface the utterance originated from (if still connected)\n608\t2. Most recently active connected surface\n609\t3. Any connected surface\n610\t4. Telegram (always-available fallback)\n611\t\n612\tException-class results (urgency: critical, type: exception) push to Telegram regardless of canvas state if no canvas has been active within the past N minutes.\n613\t\n614\t---\n615\t\n616\t## Relationship to Existing Infrastructure\n617\t\n618\taide-de-camp adds a routing and rendering layer on top of existing infrastructure without replacing any of it:\n619\t\n620\t- **NEEDLE workers** — unchanged. Task beads work exactly as before. The bead watcher is a new daemon that observes bead closure events.\n621\t- **telegram-claude-bridge** — reused for Telegram surface and HUMAN bead push delivery.\n622\t- **whisper-stt** — already deployed on ardenone-cluster; available as STT backend.\n623\t- **ZAI proxy** — all direct API calls (router, synthesize, escalate) route through `llm-proxy.ardenone.com`.\n624\t- **claude-governor** — governs subscription token consumption for self-modification and UI-regen task beads.\n625\t- **DUCK-E** — FastAPI scaffolding, WebSocket handling, OpenAI Realtime API session management, and middleware reused as the voice layer foundation.\n626\t- **beads (br CLI)** — task work items and HUMAN exceptions only. Conversational session state lives in a separate SQLite session store.\n627\t- **kubectl proxies** — fetch strand uses existing kubectl proxy access per cluster.\n628\t- **ArgoCD read-only proxy** — fetch strand reads ArgoCD application state via `argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444`.\n629\t\n630\tNet-new code: approximately 1,400 lines of wiring across router, strand waterfall, bead watcher, session store, and canvas frontend.\n631\t\n632\t---\n633\t\n634\t## Security Model\n635\t\n636\t- aide-de-camp runs on the Hetzner server; all cluster access via read-only kubectl proxies (same as existing tooling)\n637\t- No cluster credentials stored by aide-de-camp; existing proxy infrastructure holds them\n638\t- Self-modification agent writes only to the `prompts/` and `config/` directories within the repo\n639\t- All artifact changes go through diff-review before application\n640\t- Rollback available for any artifact via version history\n641\t\n642\t---\n643\t\n644\t## Open Questions\n645\t\n646\t1. **Routing accuracy** — how reliably can a haiku-class model split a rambling multi-project utterance into clean tagged threads? What's the false-split / under-split rate in practice?\n647\t\n648\t2. **Context latency** — can the fetch strand reliably hit kubectl + ArgoCD + git + beads within a 2-3s window? Which sources need timeouts and what's the degraded-result behavior?\n649\t\n650\t3. **Component selection** — when does the UI-regen agent generate a new component vs. stretch an existing one? How is \"good enough\" match defined?\n651\t\n652\t4. **Concurrency budget** — how many parallel synthesize calls can the ZAI proxy handle without queue pressure affecting the <3s target?\n653\t\n654\t5. **Topic vague reference resolution** — \"the pipeline\" vs. \"options pipeline\" vs. \"options-pipeline\". When does the router resolve from context vs. ask for clarification?\n655\t\n656\t6. **Voice UX** — push-to-talk vs. continuous listening + VAD silence detection. What's the right default for the audio surface?\n657\t\n658\t7. **Disambiguation flow** — when router confidence is below threshold, how does the clarification round-trip work without breaking conversational flow in audio mode?\n659\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5c009ef5-63b3-4d31-bc6d-7539d47a4ca7","uuid":"964ce7fc-419f-478f-9a35-436b2f0ec587","timestamp":"2026-07-02T13:37:06.345Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"# aide-de-camp: Application Plan\n\n## Overview\n\naide-de-camp is a universal personal interface that accepts stream-of-consciousness voice and text input, routes it to parallel agents across any domain, and renders results as a live canvas of HTML cards or as synthesized audio narration. It is built on top of existing personal infrastructure and improves itself through conversational feedback after initial bootstrap.\n\nCLI invocation: `adc`\n\n---\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nManaging multiple active projects requires constant context-switching between dedicated tabs, terminals, and chat sessions. The input channel (typing into a specific Claude Code tab) is also the routing layer, and that conflation adds cognitive overhead. The same problem extends beyond coding: research, lookups, personal context, reminders — anything that requires knowing which system to ask before you can ask it.\n\naide-de-camp eliminates that overhead by providing a single input surface that routes automatically, dispatches in parallel, and returns organized results without requiring the user to know where to ask.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architecture\n\n### The Hot Path\n\n```\nUser utterance (voice or text)\n  → STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n  → Intent Router (one LLM call, haiku-class, ~500ms)\n      output: [{project_slug, intent_type, urgency, utterance_fragment}, ...]\n  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize workers (one per intent thread)\n      Fetch: deterministic code, executes command matrix based on intent_type\n      Synthesize: one LLM call per thread (sonnet-class, ~1-2s)\n      output: {data, summary, urgency}\n  → Results streamed via SSE to active canvas\n  → Cards rendered client-side using component templates\n```\n\nTarget: < 3 seconds from utterance to first partial result on canvas. No Claude Code session startup on this path.\n\n### The Async Path\n\n```\nTask-profile intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n  → NEEDLE task bead created (br create)\n  → Existing Claude Code workers pick up bead normally\n  → Bead watcher detects closure event\n  → Result written to session store results table\n  → SSE push to active canvas (or Telegram if no canvas)\n```\n\n### The Self-Improvement Path\n\n```\nFeedback signal (explicit instruction or implicit engagement pattern)\n  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE task bead)\n      reads target artifact (prompt file, registry YAML, component template)\n      generates update\n      surfaces diff to user\n  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded on next invocation\n```\n\n---\n\n## Components\n\n### 1. Intent Router\n\n**Runtime:** Direct API call (haiku-class via ZAI proxy)\n\nOne LLM call per utterance. Receives the full utterance text, the project registry, and a segmentation prompt. Returns a JSON array of intent threads, each tagged with:\n\n```json\n[\n  {\n    \"project_slug\": \"options-pipeline\",\n    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"project_slug\": \"ibkr-mcp\",\n    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"what's the state of the ibkr mcp\"\n  }\n]\n```\n\nIntent types: `status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`\n\nThe router reads its segmentation prompt and the project registry from disk on each call — no caching. Hot-reload is automatic.\n\nAmbiguous intents: if confidence is below threshold, router returns an `intent_type: \"clarification\"` thread. The voice model or canvas handles the clarification round-trip before dispatching.\n\n### 2. Project Registry\n\n**Format:** YAML file, read per-invocation by the router\n\nDefines projects the router knows about:\n\n```yaml\nprojects:\n  options-pipeline:\n    aliases: [\"the pipeline\", \"options\"]\n    description: \"Options data pipeline on apexalgo-iad\"\n    cluster: apexalgo-iad\n    namespace: options\n    intent_support: [status, action, brainstorm]\n    workflows:\n      deploy:\n        steps: [ci-status, image-tag, argocd-sync, pod-status]\n\n  ibkr-mcp:\n    aliases: [\"ibkr\", \"the mcp\"]\n    description: \"IBKR MCP server\"\n    cluster: apexalgo-iad\n    namespace: ibkr-mcp\n    intent_support: [status, brainstorm, lookup]\n```\n\nUpdated by the self-modification agent. Hot-reloaded on every router call.\n\n### 3. Fetch Strand\n\n**Runtime:** Deterministic code, no LLM\n\nExecutes a command matrix based on `intent_type` and the project's registry entry. No LLM decisions — the commands are determined by the intent type and project config.\n\nExample command matrix for `intent_type: status`:\n\n| Source | Command |\n|--------|---------|\n| Pod status | `kubectl get pods -n {namespace}` |\n| ArgoCD sync | `curl argocd-ro-api/applications/{app}` |\n| Git log | `git -C {repo_path} log -10 --oneline` |\n| Bead list | `br list --project {slug} --status open` |\n| CI status | `kubectl get workflows -n argo-workflows -l project={slug}` |\n\nResults are structured data passed to the Synthesize strand. Fetch runs each source concurrently; partial results (from sources that respond first) are passed to Synthesize incrementally to support streaming output.\n\nA `fetch_coverage` field tracks which sources succeeded and which failed. Failed sources are surfaced as caveats in the result.\n\n### 4. Synthesize Strand\n\n**Runtime:** Direct API call (sonnet-class via ZAI proxy)\n\nTakes `{intent_spec, fetched_context}` → produces `{data, summary}`.\n\n- `data`: structured JSON matching the component library's expected schema for this intent type\n- `summary`: 1-3 sentence narration suitable for audio mode\n\nSingle-turn inference. Reads its system prompt from a file per invocation (hot-reload). The synthesize prompt is the highest-leverage artifact in the system — it defines result format, detail level, and what fields to include.\n\nRuns once per intent thread in parallel across all active threads.\n\n### 5. Escalate Strand\n\n**Runtime:** Direct API call + `br create`\n\nFor task-profile intents that need durable async handling:\n1. One LLM call to formulate a bead body from the intent + context\n2. `br create` to create the bead in the appropriate project\n3. Returns a pending-card spec with the bead reference\n\nThe bead watcher subsequently bridges bead closure to result delivery.\n\n### 6. Voice Model\n\n**Runtime:** Realtime API (OpenAI or Claude Realtime, persistent session)\n\nThe conversational layer. Receives transcribed utterances (or audio directly), calls `dispatch_intent()` tool to trigger routing, and narrates results at appropriate moments.\n\nTool calls are triggers, not queries. `dispatch_intent()` returns an acknowledgment immediately; results arrive out-of-band and are surfaced by the voice model at natural conversational pauses.\n\nThe voice model's system prompt controls narration style, batching behavior, urgency handling, and multi-turn topic tracking. Read per session-turn from a file (hot-reload).\n\nIn audio mode there is no canvas. The voice model reads `result.summary` directly. When a user transitions from audio to canvas, pending results are rendered using the component library and appear on canvas — session continuity means the canvas catches up.\n\n### 7. UI-Regen Agent\n\n**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n\nSteward of the component library. Given a result's data shape:\n1. Finds the best-fit existing component (semantic match against component descriptions)\n2. If no good fit, generates a new component from scratch and stores it\n3. Applies the component template to result data accounting for layout bucket\n4. On feedback, iterates the component (updates library, triggers canvas re-render)\n\nMulti-step: reads library, generates or selects, writes back. Claude Code's file access and tool use make this the right runtime.\n\nCreated as a task bead; not on the hot path. Canvas updates in place when a component is versioned (SSE push: `component_updated: {component_id, version}`).\n\n### 8. Self-Modification Agent\n\n**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n\nReads and writes the artifacts that encode system behavior:\n- Router prompt (`prompts/router.md`)\n- Project registry (`config/registry.yaml`)\n- Strand prompts (`prompts/fetch.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, etc.)\n- Urgency classifier prompt (`prompts/urgency.md`)\n- Voice model prompt (`prompts/voice.md`)\n- Monitoring config (`config/monitoring.yaml`)\n- Exception routing rules (`config/exceptions.yaml`)\n\nWorkflow for every modification:\n1. Read current artifact\n2. Understand intent of change\n3. Generate updated artifact\n4. Surface diff to user (in canvas or narrated in audio mode)\n5. On approval: write artifact, hot-reload takes effect on next invocation\n6. On rejection: discard\n\nSafety model:\n- Diffs before every application — no silent changes\n- All artifacts versioned; any update can be rolled back with one instruction\n- Confidence threshold: unambiguous changes (adding an alias) can auto-apply with notification; structural changes always require explicit approval\n\n### 9. Background Analysis Bead\n\n**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead, low priority)\n\nRuns on a schedule. Reads intent history and engagement signals from the session store, identifies patterns, proposes artifact updates.\n\nExamples:\n- \"User consistently drills into status results for more detail → propose adding causality fields to synthesize prompt\"\n- \"User ignores pipeline monitoring pushes for 5 days → propose lowering urgency tier\"\n- \"After deploy status, user always asks about pod logs within 2 minutes → propose speculative pre-fetch\"\n\nProposals are surfaced as cards on canvas for user review. Auto-apply only above a high confidence threshold.\n\n### 10. Bead Watcher\n\n**Runtime:** Daemon process, no LLM\n\nWatches for bead close events from NEEDLE workers. On close:\n1. Reads `session_id` from closed bead metadata\n2. Looks up active surface in session store\n3. Writes result to `results` table\n4. Fires SSE push to canvas or Telegram push if no canvas active\n\nPure I/O. Runs as a long-running process (Deployment, not a K8s Job per infrastructure conventions).\n\n---\n\n## Data Model\n\n### Session Store (SQLite)\n\n```sql\nsessions (\n  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  created_at  INTEGER,\n  last_active INTEGER,\n  primary_surface_id TEXT\n)\n\nsurfaces (\n  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  session_id      TEXT,\n  type            TEXT,  -- 'canvas' | 'telegram' | 'audio'\n  state           TEXT,  -- 'active' | 'idle' | 'disconnected'\n  always_available INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- 1 for Telegram\n  last_seen       INTEGER\n)\n\nutterances (\n  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  session_id  TEXT,\n  raw_text    TEXT,\n  created_at  INTEGER\n)\n\nintents (\n  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  utterance_id TEXT,\n  session_id   TEXT,\n  topic_id     TEXT,\n  project_slug TEXT,\n  intent_type  TEXT,\n  status       TEXT,  -- 'pending' | 'dispatched' | 'resolved' | 'cancelled'\n  bead_ref     TEXT,  -- set for task-profile intents\n  created_at   INTEGER,\n  resolved_at  INTEGER\n)\n\nresults (\n  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  intent_id   TEXT,\n  topic_id    TEXT,\n  session_id  TEXT,\n  summary     TEXT,\n  data        TEXT,  -- JSON\n  urgency     TEXT,  -- 'critical' | 'high' | 'normal' | 'low'\n  created_at  INTEGER,\n  surfaced_at INTEGER,\n  acked_at    INTEGER\n)\n\ntopics (\n  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  label        TEXT,\n  type         TEXT,  -- 'project' | 'research' | 'personal' | 'exception'\n  project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n  scope        TEXT,  -- 'session' | 'cross-session' | 'global'\n  session_id   TEXT,  -- null for cross-session/global\n  created_at   INTEGER,\n  last_active  INTEGER,\n  archived_at  INTEGER\n)\n\nintent_topics (\n  intent_id TEXT,\n  topic_id  TEXT,\n  PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id)\n)\n```\n\n### Component Library (SQLite, separate DB)\n\n```sql\ncomponents (\n  id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  name          TEXT,\n  description   TEXT,\n  html_template TEXT,\n  version       INTEGER,\n  created_at    INTEGER,\n  last_used     INTEGER,\n  usage_count   INTEGER\n)\n\ncomponent_versions (\n  component_id  TEXT,\n  version       INTEGER,\n  html_template TEXT,\n  created_at    INTEGER,\n  change_note   TEXT,\n  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, version)\n)\n\ncard_cache (\n  result_id         TEXT,\n  component_id      TEXT,\n  component_version INTEGER,\n  layout_bucket     TEXT,  -- 'compact' | 'normal' | 'expanded'\n  rendered_html     TEXT,\n  created_at        INTEGER,\n  PRIMARY KEY (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n)\n```\n\n---\n\n## File System Layout\n\n```\naide-de-camp/\n├── adc                      ← CLI entry point (shell script or compiled binary)\n├── config/\n│   ├── registry.yaml        ← project registry (hot-reloaded by router)\n│   ├── monitoring.yaml      ← ambient monitoring rules\n│   └── exceptions.yaml      ← exception routing rules\n├── prompts/\n│   ├── router.md            ← intent segmentation prompt\n│   ├── synthesize.md        ← result generation prompt\n│   ├── voice.md             ← voice model system prompt\n│   ├── urgency.md           ← urgency classification prompt\n│   └── fetch/               ← per-intent-type fetch instructions\n│       ├── status.md\n│       ├── action.md\n│       └── ...\n├── src/\n│   ├── router/              ← intent segmentation (direct API call)\n│   ├── fetch/               ← fetch strand (deterministic, per intent type)\n│   ├── synthesize/          ← synthesize strand (direct API call)\n│   ├── escalate/            ← escalate strand (bead creation)\n│   ├── watcher/             ← bead watcher daemon\n│   ├── session/             ← session store (SQLite)\n│   ├── canvas/              ← web frontend (SSE consumer, card renderer)\n│   └── cli/                 ← adc CLI\n├── data/\n│   ├── session.db           ← session store\n│   └── components.db        ← component library\n└── docs/\n    ├── research/            ← architecture and design research\n    ├── plan/\n    │   └── plan.md          ← this file\n    └── notes/\n        └── naming.md\n```\n\n---\n\n## Deployment Model\n\naide-de-camp is a **live web application**, not a static site. The FastAPI server is the intelligence layer — it handles SSE connections, maintains session state, runs the router and synthesize strands, and serves the frontend HTML. There is no CDN path (no CF Pages).\n\n### Why not static\n\n- SSE connections are long-lived; CDNs cannot proxy them\n- WebSocket for the Realtime API voice session requires a persistent backend\n- The session store (SQLite) and artifact store (prompts, registry) require a writable filesystem\n- Hot-reload depends on the running server reading updated files from disk on each invocation\n\n### Phased hosting\n\n**Phase 0 (Hetzner server directly)**\n\nRun FastAPI as a process on the Hetzner server itself, not in k8s. Simplest possible path:\n- NEEDLE workers and the aide-de-camp server share the same filesystem\n- Self-modification agent writes directly to `prompts/` and `config/` — hot-reload works without any coordination\n- Expose via Tailscale (the server is already on the mesh); no ingress config needed\n- SQLite DBs are local files; no PVC required\n\nNo container, no CI, no ArgoCD. Just `uvicorn src.main:app` managed by a long-running tmux session or systemd unit.\n\n**Phase 1+ (containerized, ardenone-cluster)**\n\nOnce session persistence and multi-surface routing are needed, containerize and move to k8s:\n\n```\nDocker image: ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp\nDeployment: ardenone-cluster, namespace: aide-de-camp\nPVC: /data/ (SATA, ReadWriteOnce)\n  /data/session.db        ← session store\n  /data/components.db     ← component library\n  /data/prompts/          ← hot-reloadable prompt files\n  /data/config/           ← registry.yaml, monitoring.yaml, exceptions.yaml\n```\n\nThe artifact store (prompts, registry) moves from the repo's working directory to the PVC. The self-modification agent updates artifacts via the aide-de-camp API (`PATCH /artifacts/{name}`), which writes to the PVC path the server reads from.\n\n### Traefik configuration for SSE and WebSocket\n\nTraefik's default response buffering breaks SSE. The IngressRoute needs:\n\n```yaml\n# IngressRoute annotations\ntraefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: aide-de-camp-strip-prefix@kubernetescrd\n\n# Middleware: disable buffering, extend timeouts\napiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\nkind: Middleware\nmetadata:\n  name: aide-de-camp-streaming\nspec:\n  buffering:\n    maxResponseBodyBytes: 0   # disable buffering\n  headers:\n    customResponseHeaders:\n      X-Accel-Buffering: \"no\"\n```\n\nServersTransport timeout:\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\nkind: ServersTransport\nmetadata:\n  name: aide-de-camp-longlived\nspec:\n  forwardingTimeouts:\n    responseHeaderTimeout: 0    # no timeout on SSE streams\n    dialTimeout: 5s\n```\n\nDUCK-E's existing WebSocket IngressRoute config is the reference implementation for this cluster.\n\n### SQLite concurrency\n\nMultiple writers (bead watcher, background analysis bead, session handler) require WAL mode to avoid lock contention:\n\n```python\n# On DB open\nconn.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\nconn.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n```\n\nWAL mode allows concurrent reads during writes and serializes multiple writers without blocking. For a personal single-user app, this is sufficient through Phase 3. A server database (Postgres) is not needed.\n\n### adc CLI connectivity\n\nThe `adc` CLI is a thin HTTP client. It talks to the running server over Tailscale:\n\n```bash\n# ~/.config/adc/config\nserver_url = \"http://aide-de-camp.ardenone.com\"   # or localhost in Phase 0\n```\n\nNo local inference. The CLI sends requests to the FastAPI backend and streams the SSE response to the terminal. On the Hetzner server in Phase 0, `server_url = \"http://localhost:8000\"`.\n\n### CI/CD (Phase 1+)\n\nNew Argo WorkflowTemplate `aide-de-camp-build` on iad-ci:\n- Docker build → `ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp`\n- Update image tag in `jedarden/declarative-config` (k8s/ardenone-cluster/aide-de-camp/)\n- ArgoCD syncs automatically\n\nSame pattern as every other containerized service in the stack.\n\n---\n\n## Technology Stack\n\n| Layer | Choice | Reason |\n|-------|--------|--------|\n| Backend | FastAPI (Python) | Reuse DUCK-E scaffolding; SSE + WebSocket support; async-native |\n| STT | Web Speech API → whisper-stt fallback | Zero-latency browser-native; whisper-stt already deployed |\n| Voice session | OpenAI Realtime API (via DUCK-E session handler) | DUCK-E scaffolding already built on this |\n| LLM calls | ZAI proxy (`llm-proxy.ardenone.com`) | Pay-per-token, no subscription pressure on hot path |\n| Task workers | NEEDLE + Claude Code | Existing infrastructure, unchanged |\n| Session store | SQLite (WAL mode) | Lightweight, local, additive-only schema; single-user app |\n| Artifact store | PVC-mounted filesystem (Phase 1+) / local filesystem (Phase 0) | Writable at runtime; hot-reload reads per-invocation |\n| Frontend | Vanilla JS + Web Components | No build pipeline; SSE consumer + client-side card renderer |\n| Phase 0 hosting | Hetzner server, local process | Simplest path; shared filesystem with NEEDLE workers |\n| Phase 1+ hosting | ardenone-cluster, k8s Deployment | Behind existing Traefik + Tailscale ingress |\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Phases\n\n### Phase 0 — Minimal Viable Surface (~2 days)\n\nValidates the core question: does routing + parallel dispatch reduce friction?\n\n- Single HTML page: textarea + mic button (Web Speech API)\n- `POST /dispatch` endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream\n- Results appear as basic cards as agents resolve\n- No persistence, no auth, no component library, no ambient push\n\nDeliverable: the core query loop working end-to-end.\n\n### Phase 1 — Session and Topics (~1 week)\n\nResults persist; the canvas has memory.\n\n- Session store (SQLite, 7 tables)\n- Topic model: canvas shows one card per active topic, updated in place\n- Telegram surface fallback (reuse telegram-claude-bridge)\n- Bead watcher: closed NEEDLE beads push results to active surface\n- Workload summary on reconnect\n- Staleness indicators on cards\n\nDeliverable: sessions that survive browser refresh; Telegram fallback working.\n\n### Phase 2 — Self-Improvement Loop (~2 weeks)\n\nThe interface can be improved by talking into it.\n\n- Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE)\n- Hot-reload confirmed at every artifact layer\n- Component library: UI-regen agent generates first components from actual result shapes\n- Canvas live-updates when a component is versioned\n- Explicit feedback processed: \"always include X\" → prompt updated → hot-reloaded\n\nDeliverable: at least one end-to-end self-modification cycle working (user instructs change, diff surfaced, approved, takes effect without redeploy).\n\n### Phase 3 — Responsiveness (~2-3 weeks)\n\nThe interface feels alive, not just reactive.\n\n- Ambient monitoring: active topics watched for state changes\n- Diff-aware results: show what changed since last result\n- Pre-warmed context: active topics refreshed in background every N minutes\n- Multi-turn within topic: follow-up questions deepen current topic context\n- Speculative pre-fetch: common follow-up patterns pre-fetched\n- Notification batching in audio mode\n- Implicit feedback signals fed to background analysis bead\n\nDeliverable: monitoring fires unprompted for a watched topic; follow-up questions are visibly faster.\n\n### Phase 4 — Audio Surface (~1-2 weeks)\n\nFull audio mode via Realtime API.\n\n- Realtime API voice session replaces text input path\n- Tool-as-trigger model: `dispatch_intent()` returns ack, result arrives async\n- Urgency-tiered voice narration\n- Audio-to-canvas session continuity\n\nDeliverable: full voice session with canvas catch-up on surface switch.\n\n---\n\n## Self-Improvement: Hot-Reload Architecture\n\nEvery artifact that encodes behavior is readable, writable, and reloaded per-invocation:\n\n| Behavior | Artifact | Reload point |\n|----------|----------|-------------|\n| Utterance segmentation | `prompts/router.md` | Each router call |\n| Intent→project routing | `config/registry.yaml` | Each router call |\n| Context fetch strategy | `prompts/fetch/{type}.md` | Each fetch invocation |\n| Result format and detail | `prompts/synthesize.md` | Each synthesize call |\n| Voice narration style | `prompts/voice.md` | Each session turn |\n| Urgency classification | `prompts/urgency.md` | Each escalate call |\n| Visual rendering | Component library (DB) | Each card render |\n| Monitoring rules | `config/monitoring.yaml` | Each monitoring tick |\n\nPer-invocation reload is the implementation strategy (read file, check mtime). File watching adds complexity for no benefit — prompts change rarely and file reads are cheap.\n\n---\n\n## Surface Routing Rules\n\nWhen a result is ready to surface, the bead watcher or synthesize strand uses this priority:\n\n1. The surface the utterance originated from (if still connected)\n2. Most recently active connected surface\n3. Any connected surface\n4. Telegram (always-available fallback)\n\nException-class results (urgency: critical, type: exception) push to Telegram regardless of canvas state if no canvas has been active within the past N minutes.\n\n---\n\n## Relationship to Existing Infrastructure\n\naide-de-camp adds a routing and rendering layer on top of existing infrastructure without replacing any of it:\n\n- **NEEDLE workers** — unchanged. Task beads work exactly as before. The bead watcher is a new daemon that observes bead closure events.\n- **telegram-claude-bridge** — reused for Telegram surface and HUMAN bead push delivery.\n- **whisper-stt** — already deployed on ardenone-cluster; available as STT backend.\n- **ZAI proxy** — all direct API calls (router, synthesize, escalate) route through `llm-proxy.ardenone.com`.\n- **claude-governor** — governs subscription token consumption for self-modification and UI-regen task beads.\n- **DUCK-E** — FastAPI scaffolding, WebSocket handling, OpenAI Realtime API session management, and middleware reused as the voice layer foundation.\n- **beads (br CLI)** — task work items and HUMAN exceptions only. Conversational session state lives in a separate SQLite session store.\n- **kubectl proxies** — fetch strand uses existing kubectl proxy access per cluster.\n- **ArgoCD read-only proxy** — fetch strand reads ArgoCD application state via `argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444`.\n\nNet-new code: approximately 1,400 lines of wiring across router, strand waterfall, bead watcher, session store, and canvas frontend.\n\n---\n\n## Security Model\n\n- aide-de-camp runs on the Hetzner server; all cluster access via read-only kubectl proxies (same as existing tooling)\n- No cluster credentials stored by aide-de-camp; existing proxy infrastructure holds them\n- Self-modification agent writes only to the `prompts/` and `config/` directories within the repo\n- All artifact changes go through diff-review before application\n- Rollback available for any artifact via version history\n\n---\n\n## Open Questions\n\n1. **Routing accuracy** — how reliably can a haiku-class model split a rambling multi-project utterance into clean tagged threads? What's the false-split / under-split rate in practice?\n\n2. **Context latency** — can the fetch strand reliably hit kubectl + ArgoCD + git + beads within a 2-3s window? Which sources need timeouts and what's the degraded-result behavior?\n\n3. **Component selection** — when does the UI-regen agent generate a new component vs. stretch an existing one? How is \"good enough\" match defined?\n\n4. **Concurrency budget** — how many parallel synthesize calls can the ZAI proxy handle without queue pressure affecting the <3s target?\n\n5. **Topic vague reference resolution** — \"the pipeline\" vs. \"options pipeline\" vs. \"options-pipeline\". When does the router resolve from context vs. ask for clarification?\n\n6. **Voice UX** — push-to-talk vs. continuous listening + VAD silence detection. What's the right default for the audio surface?\n\n7. **Disambiguation flow** — when router confidence is below threshold, how does the clarification round-trip work without breaking conversational flow in audio mode?\n","numLines":659,"startLine":1,"totalLines":659}}}
